issues.test_name: Test failed: ! => {"ansible_facts": {"discovered_interpreter_python": "/usr/libexec/platform-python"}, "changed": false,
issues.ai_summary:
---

**TL;DR:** Telco5g bare-metal cluster install failed; abort file detected, kubeconfig never produced, PTP tests never ran.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: OCP cluster installation failure on bare-metal host `ptpcimno`** (affects: all tests — none ran)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    fatal: [10.8.34.218]: FAILED! => {"path": "/home/kni/abort", "exists": true, "failed_when_result": true}
    VERSION   AVAILABLE   PROGRESSING   STATUS
              False       True          Unable to apply 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-07-19-060131: some cluster operators are not available
    ptpcimno-worker-0   provisioning   ...  (still provisioning after 45m)
    ERROR: kubeconfig not found at /tmp/secret/kubeconfig after fetch-kubeconfig
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The bare-metal telco5g cluster (`ptpcimno`) failed to complete OCP installation before the CI job ran. After 45+ minutes, worker nodes were still in `provisioning` state, cluster operators were unavailable (stuck on nightly `5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-07-19-060131`), and the cluster setup script wrote an `/home/kni/abort` sentinel file. The Ansible playbook `ocp-install.yml` task "Check if abort file exists" (line 12) detected that file and failed immediately. No kubeconfig was produced, so the entire test suite was skipped — the first domino is the failed worker BMH provisioning on the physical lab host.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — this is an infra/lab environment issue, not a code regression.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — bare-metal lab provisioning failures are transient; the abort mechanism is a safety guard against a stuck prior run or hardware/network issue during BMH provisioning.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Retry the job; if it fails repeatedly, check the telco5g lab host `10.8.34.218` / cluster `ptpcimno` for stuck BMH provisioning, stale `/home/kni/abort` files, or ironic/network issues preventing worker provisioning.
  - **Affected tests:** All PTP tests (none executed — job aborted in the `pre` cluster-setup phase)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single group; there is no second root cause. The abort file failure is both the first and only domino.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.20 (nightly 2026-07-20-153735): cluster install failed on bare-metal before any PTP tests could run.

**Affected Images:** No project images failed to build or pull; the OCP release image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.20.0-0.nightly-2026-07-20-153735` was imported successfully (189 images). The failure is in the physical lab cluster install, not in container images.

**Overall Issue Class:** infra_other

**Overall Severity:** MEDIUM

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - security.openshift.io
  resourceNames:
  - privileged
  resources:
  - securitycontextconstraints
  verbs:
  - use
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  managedFields:
  name: privileged-rights
  namespace: openshift-ptp
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: privileged-rights
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: builder
  namespace: openshift-ptp
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman
  namespace: openshift-ptp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  serviceAccountName: builder
  containers:
    - name: priv
      image: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4
      command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -xe
          yum install jq git wget podman-docker -y
          yum group install "development-tools" -y
          wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          rm -rf /usr/local/go && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.20.4.linux-amd64.tar.gz
          export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
          go version

          # set +x here to hide pass from log
          set +xe

          echo "podman login with serviceaccount"

          # Used for 4.16 and newer releases.
          pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".auth /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
          pass=`echo ${pass:1:-1} | base64 -d`
          podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:8} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false

          # Used for 4.15 and older releases.
          if ! podman login --get-login image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 &> /dev/null; then
            pass=$( jq .\"image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000\".password /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push/.dockercfg )
            podman login -u serviceaccount -p ${pass:1:-1} image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000 --tls-verify=false
          fi

          set -x

          export IMG=PTP_IMAGE
          export DAEMON_IMG="DAEMON_IMAGE"
          export SIDECAR_IMG="SIDECAR_IMAGE"

          export T5CI_VERSION="T5CI_VERSION_VAL"
          export USE_UPSTREAM="USE_UPSTREAM_VAL"

          # run latest release on upstream main branch
          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Running on upstream main branch"
            git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator.git
          else
            git clone --single-branch --branch OPERATOR_VERSION https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.git
          fi
          cd ptp-operator
          # OCPBUGS-52327 fix build due to libresolv.so link error
          sed -i "s/\(CGO_ENABLED=\${CGO_ENABLED}\) \(GOOS=\${GOOS}\)/\1 CC=\"gcc -fuse-ld=gold\" \2/" hack/build.sh
          # For UPSTREAM use Dockerfile for upstream contents
          if [[ "$T5CI_VERSION" =~ 4.1[2-8]+ || "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" Dockerfile
            make docker-build
          else
            # Dockerfile is updated to upstream in 4.19+. Use .ocp or .ci versions for non-UPSTREAM runs
            if [ -f "Dockerfile.ocp" ]; then
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ocp"
            else
              DOCKERFILE="Dockerfile.ci"
            fi
            sed -i "/ENV GO111MODULE=off/ a\ENV GOMAXPROCS=20" "$DOCKERFILE"
            podman build -t "${IMG}" -f "$DOCKERFILE"
          fi
          podman push ${IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          if [[ "${USE_UPSTREAM:-false}" == "false" ]]; then
            # If we a running a downstream run we are done
            exit 0
          fi

          # If were running upstream we should also use the upstream daemon!
          echo "Running on upstream main branch of linuxptp-daemon"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git
          cd linuxptp-daemon
          # Split DAEMON_IMG into IMAGE_TAG_BASE and VERSION because
          # hack/build-image.sh unconditionally overwrites IMG from these two vars.
          IMAGE_TAG_BASE="${DAEMON_IMG%:*}" VERSION="${DAEMON_IMG##*:}" make image
          podman push ${DAEMON_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"
          git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/redhat-cne/cloud-event-proxy.git
          cd cloud-event-proxy
          IMG=${SIDECAR_IMG} make podman-build
          podman push ${SIDECAR_IMG} --tls-verify=false
          cd ..

      securityContext:
        privileged: true
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/openshift.io/push
          name: dockercfg
          readOnly: true
        - name: secret-volume
          mountPath: /root/.docker
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: pull-secret
        items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json

    - name: dockercfg
      secret:
        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG
        defaultMode: 384
'

issues.test_name: [It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync
issues.ai_summary:
---

**TL;DR:** A test-code key collision in `createStoredEvent` (commit `d20ffcbf0c`, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **15/15 failures: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out** — commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the `StoredEventValues` map key from `v.DataType` to `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress). For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both the `notification` ("LOCKED") and `metric` (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. `ptp.go:414` then reads `values["notification"]` as `""` (key absent), `wasLocked` never becomes `true`, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a **test-code regression only**; production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

- **All suites share the same root cause** (`bc`, `oc`, and per the oc analysis also `dualfollower`, `dualnicbc`, `dualnicbcha`). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running `Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` hits the same bad key lookup path in `testSyncState`. No suite-specific variation is present — the `clockOffset=0.000000` debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the `notification` and `metric` reads.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert the map key change in `createStoredEvent`** (`test/pkg/event/event.go`, v2 path, ~line 730): restore `v.DataType` as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the `d20ffcbf0c` change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. **Add a regression test for `createStoredEvent`** that asserts the returned map contains both `"notification"` and `"metric"` keys when given a dual-DataValue `os-clock-sync-state-change` event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. **Block the PR that introduced `d20ffcbf0c`** (or open a revert PR) against `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## bc

---

### Suite: bc (1 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` commit `d20ffcbf0c` (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in `createStoredEvent` that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` drops LOCKED notification state** (affects: `[It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)

  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [event.go: 730] Context Attributes,
      type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change
      source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state
    Data,
      {"version":"1.0","values":[
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}
      ]}
    [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms
    [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]
    [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    [ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    ```

  - **Root Cause:** Commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed `createStoredEvent` in `test/pkg/event/event.go` (v2 path, line 730) to use `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress) as the `StoredEventValues` map key instead of `v.DataType`. For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both DataValues — the `notification` ("LOCKED") and the `metric` (-2) — share the **same** ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the second loop iteration (`metric`) silently overwrites the first (`notification`). The resulting map has no `"notification"` key, so `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414` always reads `state = ""` and `wasLocked` never becomes `true`. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per `{Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5}` config. This is a **test-code regression on main** — the production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

  - **Is it a flake?** No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

  - **Suggested Fix:** In `test/pkg/event/event.go` `createStoredEvent` v2 path, restore `v.DataType` as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from `d20ffcbf0c`). To also support the multiple-`metric` BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like `"<ResourceAddress>/<DataType>"` and update all callers that read `values["notification"]` and `values["metric"]` accordingly.

**Suite Issue Class:** test_regression  
**Suite Severity:** HIGH

## oc

---

### Suite: oc (1 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** The single `[oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by `ResourceAddress` (e.g. `/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15`) instead of by `DataType` (`"notification"`), so `ptp.go:414` never resolved `state == "LOCKED"` and `wasLocked` remained `false` until the 5-minute timeout fired.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized** (affects: `[It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395
    ```
    ```
    DEBUG  [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]
    DEBUG  [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    ```
    ```
    INFO   [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms
    INFO   [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex
    ```
    The `SynchronizedBeforeSuite` also shows: `PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false` at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute `BeforeEach` warmup.

  - **Root Cause:** `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go` subscribes to `OsClockSyncStateChange` events and calls `event.PushInitialEvent` to get the current clock state. The initial event IS received (a stale LOCKED event timestamped 37 seconds earlier), but its `StoredEventValues` map uses the `ResourceAddress` path (`/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REA

---

**TL;DR:** A test-code key collision in `createStoredEvent` (commit `d20ffcbf0c`, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **15/15 failures: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out** — commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the `StoredEventValues` map key from `v.DataType` to `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress). For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both the `notification` ("LOCKED") and `metric` (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. `ptp.go:414` then reads `values["notification"]` as `""` (key absent), `wasLocked` never becomes `true`, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a **test-code regression only**; production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

- **All suites share the same root cause** (`bc`, `oc`, and per the oc analysis also `dualfollower`, `dualnicbc`, `dualnicbcha`). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running `Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` hits the same bad key lookup path in `testSyncState`. No suite-specific variation is present — the `clockOffset=0.000000` debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the `notification` and `metric` reads.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert the map key change in `createStoredEvent`** (`test/pkg/event/event.go`, v2 path, ~line 730): restore `v.DataType` as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the `d20ffcbf0c` change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. **Add a regression test for `createStoredEvent`** that asserts the returned map contains both `"notification"` and `"metric"` keys when given a dual-DataValue `os-clock-sync-state-change` event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. **Block the PR that introduced `d20ffcbf0c`** (or open a revert PR) against `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

---

**TL;DR:** A test-code key collision in `createStoredEvent` (commit `d20ffcbf0c`, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **15/15 failures: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out** — commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the `StoredEventValues` map key from `v.DataType` to `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress). For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both the `notification` ("LOCKED") and `metric` (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. `ptp.go:414` then reads `values["notification"]` as `""` (key absent), `wasLocked` never becomes `true`, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a **test-code regression only**; production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

- **All suites share the same root cause** (`bc`, `oc`, and per the oc analysis also `dualfollower`, `dualnicbc`, `dualnicbcha`). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running `Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` hits the same bad key lookup path in `testSyncState`. No suite-specific variation is present — the `clockOffset=0.000000` debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the `notification` and `metric` reads.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert the map key change in `createStoredEvent`** (`test/pkg/event/event.go`, v2 path, ~line 730): restore `v.DataType` as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the `d20ffcbf0c` change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. **Add a regression test for `createStoredEvent`** that asserts the returned map contains both `"notification"` and `"metric"` keys when given a dual-DataValue `os-clock-sync-state-change` event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. **Block the PR that introduced `d20ffcbf0c`** (or open a revert PR) against `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

**TL;DR:** A test-code key collision in `createStoredEvent` (commit `d20ffcbf0c`, 2026-07-03) causes every "PTP Slave Clock Sync" event test to miss the LOCKED state and time out.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **15/15 failures: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` silently drops LOCKED notification state, causing all PTP Slave Clock Sync tests to time out** — commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (merged 2026-07-03) changed the `StoredEventValues` map key from `v.DataType` to `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress). For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both the `notification` ("LOCKED") and `metric` (-2) DataValues share the same ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the metric entry silently overwrites the notification entry. `ptp.go:414` then reads `values["notification"]` as `""` (key absent), `wasLocked` never becomes `true`, and the test times out after 5 minutes. The clock hardware was confirmed LOCKED in raw CloudEvent data — this is a **test-code regression only**; production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

- **All suites share the same root cause** (`bc`, `oc`, and per the oc analysis also `dualfollower`, `dualnicbc`, `dualnicbcha`). The failure is systematic and code-level: every suite running `Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` hits the same bad key lookup path in `testSyncState`. No suite-specific variation is present — the `clockOffset=0.000000` debug output (instead of the expected metric value) appears in both analyzed suites identically, confirming the key-mismatch affects both the `notification` and `metric` reads.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert the map key change in `createStoredEvent`** (`test/pkg/event/event.go`, v2 path, ~line 730): restore `v.DataType` as the key for each DataValue loop iteration, undoing the `d20ffcbf0c` change. This directly fixes all 15 failures across every suite.

2. **Add a regression test for `createStoredEvent`** that asserts the returned map contains both `"notification"` and `"metric"` keys when given a dual-DataValue `os-clock-sync-state-change` event — preventing future key changes from silently breaking event parsing.

3. **Block the PR that introduced `d20ffcbf0c`** (or open a revert PR) against `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main immediately, as every nightly run on this job will continue to fail 100% of the time until the fix lands.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — all 15 test failures trace to a single deterministic code regression on main; no hardware or infra issues involved, but the entire nightly job is broken on every run until resolved.

## bc

---

### Suite: bc (1 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` commit `d20ffcbf0c` (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in `createStoredEvent` that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` drops LOCKED notification state** (affects: `[It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)

  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [event.go: 730] Context Attributes,
      type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change
      source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state
    Data,
      {"version":"1.0","values":[
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}
      ]}
    [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms
    [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]
    [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    [ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    ```

  - **Root Cause:** Commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed `createStoredEvent` in `test/pkg/event/event.go` (v2 path, line 730) to use `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress) as the `StoredEventValues` map key instead of `v.DataType`. For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both DataValues — the `notification` ("LOCKED") and the `metric` (-2) — share the **same** ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the second loop iteration (`metric`) silently overwrites the first (`notification`). The resulting map has no `"notification"` key, so `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414` always reads `state = ""` and `wasLocked` never becomes `true`. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per `{Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5}` config. This is a **test-code regression on main** — the production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

  - **Is it a flake?** No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

  - **Suggested Fix:** In `test/pkg/event/event.go` `createStoredEvent` v2 path, restore `v.DataType` as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from `d20ffcbf0c`). **Suite Severity:** HIGH

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` drops LOCKED notification state** (affects: `[It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)

  - **Evidence:**

    ```
      type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change
      source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state
    Data,
      {"version":"1.0","values":[
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}
      ]}
    [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms
    [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]
    [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    [ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    ```

  - **Root Cause:** Commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed `createStoredEvent` in `test/pkg/event/event.go` (v2 path, line 730) to use `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress) as the `StoredEventValues` map key instead of `v.DataType`. For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both DataValues — the `notification` ("LOCKED") and the `metric` (-2) — share the **same** ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the second loop iteration (`metric`) silently overwrites the first (`notification`). The resulting map has no `"notification"` key, so `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414` always reads `state = ""` and `wasLocked` never becomes `true`.   - **Suggested Fix:** In `test/pkg/event/event.go` `createStoredEvent` v2 path, restore `v.DataType` as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from `d20ffcbf0c`). **Suite TL;DR:** The single bc-parallel test "PTP Slave Clock Sync" timed out unable to detect a LOCKED OS clock because a test-code change in `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` commit `d20ffcbf0c` (July 3, 2026) introduced a key collision bug in `createStoredEvent` that silently drops the notification state from received CloudEvents.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Key collision in `createStoredEvent` drops LOCKED notification state** (affects: `[It] [bc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)

  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [event.go: 730] Context Attributes,
      type: event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change
      source: /sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state
    Data,
      {"version":"1.0","values":[
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"notification","value_type":"enumeration","value":"LOCKED"},
        {"ResourceAddress":"/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME","data_type":"metric","value_type":"decimal64.3","value":"-2"}
      ]}
    [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36942 ms
    [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state ... eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:-2]]
    [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    [ptp.go: 393] [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    ```

  - **Root Cause:** Commit `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` (merged 2026-07-03, title: "CNF-19588: CI test for clockClass recovery after cloud-event-proxy crash") changed `createStoredEvent` in `test/pkg/event/event.go` (v2 path, line 730) to use `v.Resource` (ResourceAddress) as the `StoredEventValues` map key instead of `v.DataType`. For `os-clock-sync-state-change` events, both DataValues — the `notification` ("LOCKED") and the `metric` (-2) — share the **same** ResourceAddress (`/cluster/node/.../CLOCK_REALTIME`), so the second loop iteration (`metric`) silently overwrites the first (`notification`). The resulting map has no `"notification"` key, so `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:413–414` always reads `state = ""` and `wasLocked` never becomes `true`. The clock hardware was actually LOCKED (confirmed by the raw CloudEvent data), but the test could not detect it and timed out after 5 minutes per `{Enable:true FailureThreshold:1 Duration:5}` config. This is a **test-code regression on main** — the production `release-4.22` images are unaffected.

  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `d20ffcbf0c` on `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main (2026-07-03) — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/d20ffcbf0c

  - **Is it a flake?** No — the key collision is deterministic and will fail on every run until fixed.

  - **Suggested Fix:** In `test/pkg/event/event.go` `createStoredEvent` v2 path, restore `v.DataType` as the map key for each DataValue (reverting the key change from `d20ffcbf0c`). To also support the multiple-`metric` BC clock-class use case that motivated the change, either accumulate values into a slice or use a composite key like `"<ResourceAddress>/<DataType>"` and update all callers that read `values["notification"]` and `values["metric"]` accordingly.

**Suite Issue Class:** test_regression  
**Suite Severity:** HIGH

## oc

---

### Suite: oc (1 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** The single `[oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync` test timed out because the OS clock LOCKED state event was received but its values were keyed by `ResourceAddress` (e.g. `/cluster/node/cnfdf30.../CLOCK_REALTIME:15`) instead of by `DataType` (`"notification"`), so `ptp.go:414` never resolved `state == "LOCKED"` and `wasLocked` remained `false` until the 5-minute timeout fired.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Event value key mismatch — OS clock LOCKED state never recognized** (affects: `[It] [oc-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAILED] OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out
    In [It] at: /tmp/cnf-s7FwM/ptp-operator-conformance-test/test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go:393 @ 07/12/26 11:01:22.395
    ```
    ```
    DEBUG  [ptp.go: 411] got map[eventsource:/sync/sync-status/os-clock-sync-state eventtimestamp:2026-07-12 10:55:50.49315292 +0000 UTC eventtype:event.sync.sync-status.os-clock-sync-state-change eventvalues:map[/cluster/node/cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com/CLOCK_REALTIME:15]]
    DEBUG  [ptp.go: 419] clockOffset=0.000000
    ```
    ```
    INFO   [event.go: 734] Latency for the event: 36928 ms
    INFO   [event.go: 589] received term signal, exiting MonitorPodLogsRegex
    ```
    The `SynchronizedBeforeSuite` also shows: `PtpEventsIsConsumerReady= false` at start, confirming the consumer app was only ready after the 1-minute `BeforeEach` warmup.

  - **Root Cause:** `testSyncState` in `test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go` subscribes to `OsClockSyncStateChange` events and calls `event.PushInitialEvent` to get the current clock state. issues.test_name: Cluster in error state (must-gather triggered)
issues.ai_summary:
Now I have everything I need. 

---

**TL;DR:** CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (`manifest unknown`)** (affects: deployment setup / `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet wait timeout)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder
    Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...
    Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown
    make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125
    + podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false
    Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known
    ```
    Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:
    ```
    Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found
    + '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'
    + return 1
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (`FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17`) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses `set +xe` / `set -x` (without restoring `-e`), so `make docker-build` and `podman push` both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs `make deploy` pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the `PtpOperatorConfig` and the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the builder image `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
  - **Is it a flake?** No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore `set -e` after the credential-hiding `set +e` block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
  - **Affected tests:** `e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests` (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.

**Per-Version Notes:**
- 4.17: confirmed — `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` returns `manifest unknown`
- 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` (missing); `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17` (never built/pushed)

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

          echo "Running on main branch of cloud-event-proxy"

        secretName: BUILDER_DOCKERCFG

issues.test_name: Cluster in error state (must-gather triggered)
issues.ai_summary:
Now I have everything I need. 

---

**TL;DR:** CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (`manifest unknown`)** (affects: deployment setup / `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet wait timeout)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder
    Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...
    Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown
    make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125
    + podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false
    Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known
    ```
    Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:
    ```
    Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found
    + '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'
    + return 1
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (`FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17`) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses `set +xe` / `set -x` (without restoring `-e`), so `make docker-build` and `podman push` both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs `make deploy` pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the `PtpOperatorConfig` and the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the builder image `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
  - **Is it a flake?** No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore `set -e` after the credential-hiding `set +e` block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
  - **Affected tests:** `e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests` (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.

**Per-Version Notes:**
- 4.17: confirmed — `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` returns `manifest unknown`
- 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` (missing); `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17` (never built/pushed)

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

issues.ai_summary:
I now have all the data needed. 

---

**TL;DR:** Worker node `cnfdf30` was `NotReady/Unknown` at test start; pre-flight check aborted before any PTP tests ran.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Unhealthy cluster node prevented test execution** (affects Tests: `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream-telco5g-ptp-tests`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com   NotReady,SchedulingDisabled   worker   15m
    NOT_READY_NODES='cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com Unknown'
    [ERROR] All nodes must be Ready before starting PTP tests. Aborting.
    + exit 1
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test step's pre-flight script (`telco5g-ptp-tests`) checks that all cluster nodes are `Ready` before launching PTP tests. Worker node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` was in `NotReady,SchedulingDisabled` with condition `Unknown` at the time the test pod started. The script detected this via `oc get nodes` and exited 1 immediately — no PTP tests ran at all. The node being in `SchedulingDisabled/Unknown` indicates it was either mid-reboot, cordoned by a prior step, or lost contact with the control plane.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — this is a transient infrastructure/cluster state issue.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — node `NotReady/Unknown` at test start time is a classic transient infra flake caused by hardware or network instability in the bare-metal telco5g pool.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Re-run the job; if the node is consistently unhealthy, investigate the bare-metal host `cnfdf30` in the telco5g pool and re-provision or replace it. Optionally extend the pre-flight check to wait/retry for node readiness instead of immediately aborting.
  - **Affected tests:** All PTP tests (none ran; job aborted at pre-flight)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — one bad cluster node. No code regression involved.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.22 (nightly 2026-06-07-152904) — cluster infrastructure issue on bare-metal telco5g pool; unrelated to the OCP version itself.

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-07-152904` (successfully imported; not the cause)

**Overall Issue Class:** infra_other

**Overall Severity:** MEDIUM
